expected_trades_per_day_basis: R6 baseline ~120 events over 30-day window ÷ 30 = 4/day; taker_buy_ratio_5m>=0.55 filter retains roughly the long-biased subset which on R6 (180s 8-15% extreme moves) is typically 60-80% of events, so estimate stays close to 3-4/day net.

best_in: BTC trending up days with funding negative or near-zero
alt-led rally regimes where R6 fires on capitulation-short squeezes
sessions with rising taker-buy aggression (Asia→EU handoff, post-CPI relief rallies)
worst_in: BTC strong downtrend days where R6 8-15% spikes are predominantly down-side (long bias misaligned)
low-vol chop where R6 barely fires (sample drops to <1/day, statistics unreliable)
high-funding-positive regimes where taker buying is already crowded longs about to unwind
regime_sensitivity.explanation: R6 geometry is more directional than the pump channels per metric_critic, and a long-only filter on taker_buy_ratio_5m>=0.55 captures genuine demand-led extreme moves. It will misfire badly when R6 fires are dominated by waterfall-down events with dead-cat-bounce taker buys, which is the structural failure mode.

live_readiness_caveats: R6 sparse trigger count (~120/30d baseline) means any single bad week of 5-10 trades materially shifts realized expectancy — do not compound size for at least 2 full paper-shadow windows.

taker_buy_ratio_5m is computed on Binance perp aggTrades; if your live feed lags >3s the 5m window slides and the filter no longer matches backtest — verify ratio computation parity before going live.
R6 events fire on micro-cap and mid-cap symbols in the 180s 8-15% band; expect 30-80bp slippage on entry market orders for the bottom 30% by ADV — paper-shadow MUST simulate slippage by ADV bucket.

monitoring_metrics.rationale: R6 is sparse; if frequency drifts far from ~4/day, either the regime changed (R6 channel quieter) or the taker_buy_ratio_5m filter is computed differently in live than in backtest.
E126's R2 paper-shadow trap (0.3946 backtest → -13.5% paper) was caught by median erosion; for R6 long the same erosion would be the earliest live signal of left-tail asymmetry.

monitoring_metrics.alert_threshold: distribution shifts so >20% of fills fire at ratio in [0.55, 0.58]
monitoring_metrics.rationale: If trades cluster at the filter boundary, the edge is marginal and feed-latency noise dominates — the archetype is no longer behaving as the backtest defined it.
At 4 trades/day, 5 consecutive losses is a ~1.25 day event; on a long-bias R6 strategy this is the signature of a BTC down-day regime mismatch and should pause sizing.

monitoring_metrics.alert_threshold: realized p25_capped_tail more negative than backtest p25_capped_tail by >50%
monitoring_metrics.rationale: This is the metric the synthesizer added specifically to catch the E126-style asymmetric TP/SL trap — must be tracked live or the lesson is wasted.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: 7-day rolling Sharpe < 0 AND median trade % flips negative for 3 consecutive days
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: R6 long edge depends on R6 fires being demand-led; if both Sharpe and median go negative simultaneously, the channel mix has rotated to waterfall-down events and the long bias is structurally wrong, not just unlucky.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: Realized max drawdown on $1000 paper account exceeds 8% within first 2 paper-shadow weeks
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: At 5-10% per-trade sizing, 8% DD ≈ 1.5-2 full losers concentrated; given R6 sparsity this is statistically loud and matches the E126 compounding-failure pattern user already paid to learn once.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: taker_buy_ratio_5m feed parity check fails (live vs backtest replay diverge by >5% on >10% of fills)
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: If the filter itself is mis-computed in live, the strategy isn't this archetype anymore — kill until parity is restored, do not 'tune through' it.

expected_max_dd_basis: R6 long with ~4 trades/day at 5-10% sizing over a 14d paper window ≈ 56 trades; assuming win rate 55-65% (R6 directional) and TP/SL near 1:1 from fixed-exit family, a 4-5 trade losing cluster is ~1-in-15 probability and produces 6-7% account DD before recovery.
compare_to_existing: Closest live/top archetype is E126 pc_pump_taker_buy_extreme_long (same taker_buy structural thesis, opposite channel) — triangulate by expecting r6_bigmove_simple_long to show LOWER backtest score but HIGHER paper-shadow realization rate, because R6 geometry is less prone to the asymmetric-TP/SL trap that destroyed E126's paper P&L.
